title: HKM Chemist - WhatsApp B2B Ordering Bot
client_name: Harikrupa Chemist, Maharaja Medical & Karnavati Agencies
short_description: Streamlined B2B ordering for pharmaceutical retailers directly on WhatsApp, automating manual catalog search, scheme calculations, and order creation.
full_description:
HKM Chemist is a comprehensive conversational commerce system designed to automate B2B ordering for pharmaceutical retailers. Instead of placing orders through manual calls or complex ERP portals, retailers search catalog items, check stock levels, view outstanding balances, and checkout directly on WhatsApp.

The system integrates seamlessly with the client's back-office systems, verifying dealer registration, checking schemes, and generating UPI payment QR codes dynamically.
challenges: Managing stateful multi-step conversation flows inside the stateless WhatsApp webhook environment was a major challenge. Additionally, validating stock availability and complex scheme rules (e.g. "Buy 10 Get 1 Free" with partial cart upgrades) required high-performance backend calculations to avoid lag in chat replies.
solution: We built a robust Django-based session router that acts as a state machine for each customer phone number. Using optimized PostgreSQL queries, the system instantly matches typed queries against tens of thousands of medicines, presenting schemes and stock options in clean, numbered list replies. Out-of-stock items trigger automated notifications directly to the owner for immediate fulfillment.

timeline.description: Mapped client ERP catalog data to design search indices and developed the Django session state machine supporting persistent carts.
Integrated Gupshup WhatsApp APIs and bank UPI QR codes, completed retailer UAT, and deployed live on Railway Cloud.

testimonial_text: This system revolutionized our B2B order flow. Retailers who used to call us or write orders manually are now placing 100% of their requests via WhatsApp. Manual order entry errors have dropped by 90%, and we process orders 3 times faster.

gallery.caption: WhatsApp Chatbot ordering flow simulator showing product search and quantity input.

title: Prishva Fintech - Multi-Role Loan CRM & Portal
short_description: A multi-role digital financial services portal and customer relationship management (CRM) platform bridging loan eligibility calculations, public customer enquiries, internal telecaller routing, partner workflows, and administrative pipeline oversight.
full_description:
Prishva Fintech is a high-performance CRM and portal built to streamline loan lead management, tracking, and conversions. The platform connects customers, agents, brokers (partners), telecallers, and administrators under a unified multi-role dashboard.

From public EMI and SIP eligibility calculators that auto-capture leads, to secure partner portals with document vaults and audit trails, Prishva Fintech automates the entire loan origination pipeline.
challenges: Managing role-based access control and strict data privacy was critical: telecallers should only log notes and follow-ups, partners should only see self-submitted leads, and clients must only see their own matching lender results and documents. Keeping page loads under 1 second while performing MySQL database joins was another priority.
solution: We developed a custom Django role-based permission system and middleware. We built a lender matching engine that calculates matching scores based on profile parameters. All static styles were designed with a custom modular FinTech CSS framework to maximize visual consistency, performance, and cross-device responsiveness.

timeline.description: Created interactive financial calculators and landing pages capturing lead parameters directly into the database.
Built permission-bounded dashboards for Customers, Partners, Telecallers, and Admins alongside document vault audit logging.
Completed the lender match scoring engine, optimized MySQL database queries, and launched the live portal.

testimonial_text: Prishva Fintech CRM has automated our entire loan application funnel. Partners submit leads directly and upload documents to client vaults, while our internal telecallers schedule call notes. Smart lender matching has boosted approvals by 45%!

gallery.caption: Administrator CRM Pipeline tracking lead statuses and loan application stages.

title: CricManager - Cricket Tournament Management System
short_description: An interactive Cricket Tournament Management and Auction system with real-time bidding dashboards, dynamic team roster management, and automated email notifications.
full_description:
CricManager is a comprehensive tournament administration and player auction platform built to streamline sports event management. Organizers host live player bidding rounds where team representatives buy players from categorized categories under strict budget constraints.

The system features real-time AJAX budget updates, asynchronous player selection, dynamic captains assignments, and multi-role dashboards for administrators, tournament organizers, and view-only public viewers.
challenges: Handling high-velocity asynchronous actions during live player bidding caused database race conditions and latency issues. Query bottlenecks from loading captain profiles inside team loops (N+1 queries) also degraded dashboard loading times.
solution: We resolved query latencies by implementing Django select_related('captain') optimizations on relational joins. A custom jQuery client-side state manager was built to sync and recalculate budgets, unsold boards, and team counts instantly without database page reloads, ensuring 100% data integrity during live auctions.

timeline.description: Designed the tournament database tables, category base prices, and multi-role permission decorators.
Built the interactive jQuery AJAX bidding dashboard with automatic budget validation check limits.
Integrated SMTP transaction notifications, CSV/Excel template import and export tools, and launched live.

testimonial_text: CricManager completely automated our tournament auction. Team captains were bought and assigned instantly, and the real-time budget synchronization meant zero manual errors during fast-paced bidding. Email updates kept players and sponsors fully informed.

gallery.caption: CricManager live auction dashboard showing real-time bid updates and team roster statuses.

title: The Great Heist - Top-Down Action Stealth Game
short_description: A top-down 2D tactical stealth action game featuring procedurally generated levels, a custom Canvas rendering loop, sound effects, and Firebase progression sync.
full_description:
The Great Heist is a fast-paced 2D stealth game compiled in Kotlin using Android Studio. Players control an operative navigating secure installations, evading moving AI guards, collecting treasure gold, and executing clean extractions.

The game implements a custom 2D game loop, twin-stick motion coordinate handlers, low-latency audio playbacks, and real-time cloud save progression synchronization using Firebase.
challenges: The continuous high-frequency allocation of Paint, Vector, and Matrix objects inside the Canvas draw() thread (running 60 times/sec) caused heavy Garbage Collection (GC) pauses and memory-leak crashes on older Android viewports.
solution: We re-architected the asset lifecycle to use a shared static bitmap cache, loading sprites only once at launch. Object instantiation was eliminated from the draw loop through static pre-allocation. Explicit .recycle() hooks were added to clear high-resolution resource streams immediately after scaling.

timeline.description: Implemented custom game loop, collision physics, and top-down AI pathfinding scripts.
Integrated Firebase Realtime Database for progression, and SoundPool for low-latency effects.
Re-allocated matrix pointers out of frame-loop, resolved GC stuttering, and compiled Android binaries.

testimonial_text: The transition from a Pygame prototype to a full Android Canvas application was challenging. Solving the garbage collection stutter and optimizing memory allocation helped us build a smooth 60 FPS mobile stealth game.

gallery.caption: The Great Heist top-down stealth gameplay showing procedurally generated rooms and guard patrol lines.
